results.title: 7 Best Circle Alternatives for Communities (2026)
results.content:
Published April 21, 2026

> Quick answer
>
> The best Circle alternatives for online communities in 2026 are Skool ($99/month, best gamified community + courses), Talkspresso (free, best for live session delivery alongside community), Mighty Networks ($33-$119/month, best branded app), Discord (free, best for informal communities), Slack (free-$12.50/user, best for professional communities), Kajabi ($149-$399/month, best all-in-one with courses + community), and Heartbeat ($49-$299/month, best for brand-focused communities). Circle is the strongest dedicated community platform, but many creators find that community is a feature, not a product. The real revenue often comes from live sessions, courses, or coaching delivered to community members, not from community access fees alone. [...] > Quick answer: The best Circle alternative depends on your real need. If it is community with courses, choose Skool ($99/month). If it is live session delivery (coaching, workshops, 1:1 calls), choose Talkspresso (free). If it is a free community without paywalls, choose Discord. Circle remains the best dedicated community platform at $49-$199/month, but many creators discover they need session tools more than community tools.

Key takeaways from this comparison: [...] | Platform | Monthly Cost | Community | Courses | Live Video | Session Tools | Gamification | Mobile App | Best For |
 ---  ---  ---  --- 
| Circle | $49-$199 | Yes (best) | Yes | Events | No | No | Web | Professional communities |
| Skool | $99 | Yes | Yes | No | No | Yes | Web | Gamified communities |
| Talkspresso | $0 | No | No | Yes (HD) | Yes (full) | No | Web | Live session delivery |
| Mighty Networks | $33-$119 | Yes | Yes | Events | No | No | Yes (branded) | Branded app communities |
| Discord | $0 | Yes (chat) | No | Voice chat | No | Bots | Yes | Real-time communities |
| Slack | $0-$12.50 | Yes (chat) | No | Huddles | No | No | Yes | Professional communities |
| Kajabi | $149-$399 | Yes | Yes | Limited | Basic | No | Web | All-in-one platform |

results.title: 6 Best Circle Alternatives for Creators (2026) — Podia
results.content:
Podia: Best all-in-one Circle alternative for building a full business along with your community
 Mighty Networks: Best community-focused platform with a lower starting price than Circle
 Bettermode: Best for creating a free community with customer support features
 Discord: Best for hosting casual chat forums on your own server
 Kajabi: Good all-in-one product and community tool if you only have a small number of products
 Facebook groups: Best community tool for getting discovered by new people and growing a free group

My recommendation? If you’re starting an online business, go with an all-in-one tool like Podia. [...] Cons: Podia isn’t the best fit if you’re looking for code-level customizations or if you want to fully DIY your setup with integrations. Podia also doesn’t have the option to build a branded community app, so if this is a priority, you may need other tools.

## #2 Mighty Networks — community-focused Circle alternative

Like Circle, Mighty Networks is a community-centered platform that lets you build courses and create other interactive experiences for your members. Every plan includes unlimited members, spaces, and moderators, so you can structure your community however you like. [...] That said, if you know you only want to run a community and you’ve already got your marketing and audience locked in, this might not be an issue. But if you’re building a different kind of online business, these alternatives to Circle are worth a look.

## #1 Podia — Best all-in-one business and community alternative to Circle

Podia is an all-in-one Circle alternative where you can build a community alongside your online courses, digital products, website, email marketing, affiliates, and everything else you need to grow.

results.title: Best Alternatives to Circle: 7 Platforms for Communities, Courses, and Memberships | eCommerce Website Design Gallery & Tech Inspiration
results.content:
## 7 of the Best Alternatives to Circle

The best Circle alternative depends on what is missing for you. Here are seven platforms that outperform Circle in one way or another:

### 1. Mighty Networks – Closest All-Round Replacement

mighty networks homepage

If you want a platform that does roughly what Circle does but with a longer track record and more built-in features, Mighty Networks is the most direct competitor. It combines community spaces, courses, events, live streaming, and private messaging in a single platform, and it has been doing so longer than most rivals. [...] ### 5. Heartbeat – Modern Community OS

heartbeat homepage

Heartbeat is a newer entrant that has quickly positioned itself as a direct Circle competitor, with transparent pricing and a modern feature set that matches most of what Circle offers: structured spaces, events, courses, chat, and automation tools.

What sets Heartbeat apart is its approach to pricing and automation. The Starter plan begins at $40/month (annual billing) for up to 1,000 members, which undercuts Circle’s entry price. The Growth plan at $108/month unlocks unlimited members and more advanced automation. The pricing is straightforward, with no hidden per-member surcharges.

results.title: Top 5 Circle.so Alternatives for Growing Communities
results.content:
Alternative

What Makes It Stand Out

Forj

AMS-ready, purpose-built for associations and professional networks, unifies community, learning, and behavioral analytics in one member experience

Higher Logic

AMS connections, strong email automation, and well-established in the association space

Hivebrite

Flexible member directories, event tools, and customizable community portals for alumni and professional networks

Mighty Networks

Course and community bundles, mobile-first design, strong for independent networks and coaches

Breezio

AI-powered content discovery, knowledge-base focus, built for professional peer learning

Each of these alternatives to Circle serves a different niche. Your ideal pick depends on your engagement goals and the kind of experience your members expect. [...] The best partners will work with you to import courses and content, map member profiles, and test connections before you launch the new solution.

It's best to ask your vendor for a tailored plan with realistic milestones.

### Are There Open-Source Circle Alternatives?

There are a few open-source Circle alternatives, such as Discourse and Flarum. They focus on forums but lack the robust learning, credentialing, and analytics features that associations and professional networks need.

You'd also carry the full burden of hosting and security, which makes a managed solution more practical for most organizations.

### What Are the Main Limitations of Circle for Associations? [...] The following alternatives to Circle.so offer distinct strengths for associations and professional networks.

### 1. Forj

Forj-1 Homepage

Forj-1 Homepage

Forj brings community, learning, and behavioral analytics together in one unified member experience built for associations and professional networks.

Where most solutions treat community and learning as separate features, Forj connects them into a single solution.

For example, your members can move from structured coursework into peer conversations that reinforce what they just learned.

Your members get a faster, smoother experience because everything lives in one place. They earn credentials, join discussion groups, and discover new courses without leaving the branded environment.
